t_ch: 绘世学院的校园怪谈有七个，它不是其中之一。
t_en: There are seven urban legends in Graphia Academy, and it is not one of them.

t_ch: 推门而入。
t_en: Push open the door and enter.

t_ch: 轻轻敲门。
t_en: Knock gently.

t_ch: 意料之中的毫无回应。但许是受到环境的影响，你心中莫名浮动起某种微妙的情感——就好像你真是个打算逃课的学生似的。
t_en: As expected, there was no response. Perhaps influenced by the environment, an oddly peculiar feeling wells up inside you, as if you really were a student skipping class.

t_ch: 算了，想那么多做什么，又不会有人在门后等着抓你。
t_en: Oh, whatever. It's not like someone's waiting to catch you behind the door.

t_ch: 转身离开。
t_en: Turn around and leave.

t_ch: 喂，你！站住，说你呢——
t_en: Hey, you! Stop right there, I'm talking to you!

t_ch: 无故逃课、擅自离校，还殴打纪律委员…你是几年几班的，学号是多少？我给你加点学分。
t_en: Skipping class willy-nilly, leaving school without permission, and beating up the disciplinary committee member... Which class and year are you in? What's your student ID? I'm logging all these to your record.

t_ch: 我不是学生…
t_en: I'm not a student...

t_ch: 门这边是二维市，门那边是绘世学院。你从门那边来却说自己不是学生，不觉得太苍白无力了吗？
t_en: This side of the door is Duomension City, and that side is the Graphia Academy. Don't you think that's a really weak excuse for someone coming from the academy side?

t_ch: 我是学生家长。
t_en: I'm a parent.

t_ch: 家长？你是家长，我还是校长呢。
t_en: Parent? Well, if you're a parent, then I'm the principal.

t_ch: 我是…银河球棒侠！
t_en: I am... the Galactic Baseballer!

t_ch: 你一定是二年级的吧？一般来说，这种症状出现在中学二年级比较多。
t_en: You must be an eighth-grader, right? I've heard about this infamous syndrome of yours.

t_ch: 国有国法，校有校规。无论你怎样巧舌如簧，都免不了受到校规的惩罚。
t_en: As the nation has its laws, so does the school its rules. Talk all you want with that silver tongue of yours, punishment will be delivered.

t_ch: 惩罚就是由你代替我，担任纪律委员一职，到放学为止。
t_en: And that punishment is... for you to take my place as the disciplinary committee member until school ends.

t_ch: 这不是惩罚，是奖励吧？
t_en: That's not a punishment. That's a reward... right?

t_ch: 你能这么想，也好。
t_en: Well, if that's what you want to believe.

t_ch: 可你的样子，有点可怕。
t_en: But you look kinda scary.

t_ch: 原本我也是一个青春貌美的学生干部，是对逃课的学生的怨念，让我变成了这副模样。
t_en: I used to be a beautiful student leader in my prime, but my grudge against students who skip classes turned me into... this thing.

t_ch: 这扇门前些天突然凭空出现在学校里，搬也搬不走、毁又毁不掉，虽然没什么危险，但格外助长了学生逃课的嚣张气焰。
t_en: This door suddenly appeared out of nowhere at school a few days ago. It can't be moved or destroyed, and while not dangerous, it's definitely encouraging students to skip school with unprecedented audacity.

t_ch: 虽说校方已经联络了专业人士来处理，但都折腾好几天了，也没见什么成效。
t_en: The higher-ups have contacted professionals to handle the matter, but they've been at it for days now without any progress.

t_ch: 教导主任为了守护他岌岌可危的发际线，干脆就把压力给到我们学生会。最近我们轮流值守，但还是有不少学生蒙混过关。
t_en: The dean of students, in a desperate attempt to keep his hairline from receding further, decided to dump all the pressure on us in the student council. We've been taking turns for patrols lately, but there are still students who managed to slip through the cracks.

t_ch: 你战胜了我，说明你比我更强，你能抓住我抓不住的同学，更有资格肩负起纪律委员的职责。
t_en: You've defeated me, that means you're stronger than I am. You can catch students that I can't, so, you're more qualified to shoulder the responsibilities of the disciplinarian.

t_ch: 至于我嘛，来都来了，就去市区逛逛再回学校吧——
t_en: As for me, well, since I'm already here, I might as well check out the city before heading back...

t_ch: 在我眼皮底下逃课？
t_en: Skipping class right under my nose?

t_ch: 抱歉，我不能包庇。
t_en: Sorry, I can't cover this up for you.

t_ch: 这么快就进入状态了吗？你果然强得可怕！
t_en: Already taking up the mantle? You really are a force to be reckoned with!

t_ch: 好吧，那么现在换我受罚，惩罚的内容是…由我来继续担任纪律委员。
t_en: Alright then, now it's my turn to be punished, and my punishment is... to continue serving as the disciplinary committee member.

t_ch: 已经把这辈子有趣的事情都想了个遍，怎么还没有放学？
t_en: I've already daydreamed about every single fun thing I could possibly do in life, and class is STILL not dismissed?

t_ch: 这是一扇阿门。
t_en: This is an A-Door.

t_ch: 推门回去。
t_en: Push open the door and go back.

t_ch: 转身离开。
t_en: Turn around and leave.

t_ch: 咚咚咚——
t_en: *knock knock*

t_ch: 这不是你见过的第一扇阿门，却是你见过的第一扇自带音效的阿门。
t_en: This isn't the first A-Door you've seen, but it's certainly the first that comes with its own sound effects.

t_ch: 咚、咚、咚——
t_en: *knock* *knock* *knock*

t_ch: 你不知道门后有什么，但你知道它似乎很急切、也很执着。
t_en: You don't know what's behind the door, but you can tell it's both urgent and persistent.

t_ch: 咚、咚、咚——
t_en: *knock* *knock* *knock*

t_ch: 许是由于久久未能得到回应，敲门声慢了下去，却越来越重……
t_en: Perhaps due to the lack of response, the knocking slowed down, yet grew heavier and heavier...
*THUMP* *THUMP* *THUMP*!

t_ch: 轻轻敲门。
t_en: Knock gently.

t_ch: 来自对面的敲击声沉寂片刻，又重新响了起来，欢欣而迫切——
t_en: The knocking fell silent for a moment, then started up again, joyful and urgent.

t_ch: 咚咚咚——咚咚咚咚咚——
t_en: *knock knock knock* *knock knock knock knock knock*

t_ch: 推门而入。
t_en: Push open the door and enter.

t_ch: 转身离开。
t_en: Turn around and leave.

t_ch: 你环观四周，却发现此处空无一人。你想起了某些二相乐园怪谈，夜深之时，伪人会敲响独居者的房门，而被拜访的住户，从第二天起会变成「另一个人」……
t_en: You look around, but find no one here. You recall a Planarcadia urban legend: In the dead of night, imposters would knock on the doors of those who live alone, and on the next day, the visited residents would become "someone else"...

t_ch: 你警惕起来，敲门声却不依不饶。
t_en: You became alert, but the knocking persists relentlessly.

t_ch: 咚咚咚——咚、咚、咚——
t_en: *knock knock knock* *knock* *knock* *knock*

t_ch: 重重敲门。
t_en: Knock on the door. Hard.

t_ch: 面对你挑衅般的回应，这次的敲门声变得格外礼貌，不徐不疾、富有韵律。
t_en: In response to your provocative reply, the knocking turns surprisingly polite and gently paced, even rhythmic.

t_ch: 咚咚——咚、咚、咚——
t_en: *knock knock* *knock* *knock* *knock*

t_ch: 如果这扇「阿门」对面真有伪人，至少也是一位有礼貌的伪人。
t_en: If there really is an impostor behind this A-Door, at least it's a polite one.

t_ch: 推门而入。
t_en: Push open the door and enter.

t_ch: 转身离开。
t_en: Turn around and leave.

t_ch: 你的脚下是软的，眼前是白的——
t_en: You feel softness beneath your feet, and you see white all around you...

t_ch: 阿门不知何时悄然退场，新晋的演员正隔着医务室的纱帘，隐约映出个似人的轮廓。
t_en: You have no idea when the A-Door had quietly disappeared. A new actor is standing behind the infirmary's curtains, casting a vague human silhouette.

t_ch: 你还在思考来者是谁，隔帘便被唰地拉开。
t_en: While you're still busy wondering who they are, the curtain suddenly pulls open.

t_ch: …等等，这一幕，我似曾相识。
t_en: ...Wait, I've seen this before.

t_ch: 我好像也有点混乱。
t_en: I'm kinda confused too.

t_ch: 你听我说…
t_en: So, listen...

t_ch: 事情不是你想的那样。
t_en: It's not what you think.

t_ch: 应该…还是如我想的那样。
t_en: It should be... just as I thought.

t_ch: 是吗？
t_en: Really?

t_ch: 看来你已经搞清楚事情来龙去脉了。
t_en: Looks like you've figured out what's going on here.

t_ch: 冷静，你听我说——
t_en: Calm down, let me explain...

t_ch: 你就是我，我就是你。没错…吧？
t_en: I am you, and you are me. That's right... right?

t_ch: 你问我，我问谁去啊？
t_en: You're asking me, but who am I supposed to ask?

t_ch: 说到底…
t_en: To be honest...

t_ch: 我对自己的精神状况也没有那么大的信心。你也很清楚，对吧？
t_en: I'm not that confident about my mental state, either. You know that, don't you?

t_ch: 总之，我是推开了一扇门之后来到这里的。在脑子回过味之前，我就打算回去了。
t_en: Long story short, I got here after opening a door. Before my brain could even process what was happening, I was already planning my way back.

t_ch: 站在地上的你深深地看了你一眼，谢幕般朝着帘子伸出了手——
t_en: The standing you takes a deep look at you, then reaches for the curtain as if closing the final act...

t_ch: 怪诞演出到此为止，短暂的交汇过后，观众与演员重归原本的轨迹，而你自始至终都是你。
t_en: The bizarre performance comes to an end. After a brief convergence, the audience and actors return to their original trails, and you remain who you've always been.

t_ch: 无论你是过去、未来、或者是幻造出来的我，拉上帘子，我们重归各自的世界线。
t_en: Whether you're from the past, the future, or an imagenated version of me... Draw the curtains, and let's each return to our own timelines.

t_ch: 这是一扇阿门，但这不是重点；重点是它在的位置——就在酒馆的角落里。
t_en: This is an A-Door, but that's not the focus here. The focus is where the door's located: right in the corner of the tavern.

t_ch: 制门匠到底有多久没出过门了？
t_en: How long has it been since the Doormaker last went out?

t_ch: 推门而入。
t_en: Push open the door and enter.

t_ch: 轻轻敲门。
t_en: Knock gently.

t_ch: 门后隐约有声音传来。那是一阵欢快的旋律，和你在真人秀里听到的音效别无二致。
t_en: You hear faint sounds coming from behind the door. An upbeat melody, no different from the sound effects you'd hear in variety game shows.

t_ch: 这扇门可能会通往一个*星途璀璨*的未来。
t_en: This door may lead you toward a future of stardom.

t_ch: 转身离开。
t_en: Turn around and leave.

t_ch: 面前有两扇门，一扇是阿门，另一扇也是阿门。
t_en: There are two doors in front of you. An A-Door, and another A-Door.

t_ch: 正在你疑惑之际，一阵欢快、愉悦的旋律响起。
t_en: Just as you're pondering in confusion, a cheerful, delightful melody starts playing.

t_ch: 噢，又一位「真真假假大竞答」的挑战者！{F#她}{M#他}能够答对题目，赢走奖池中的所有奖金，还是推开错误时间线的大门，永远迷失？让我们拭目以待！
t_en: Oh, here comes another challenger for the "True or False Quiz Show"! Will {F#she}{M#he} answer correctly and take home the jackpot? Or will {F#she}{M#he} open the door to the wrong timeline and become lost forever? Let's wait and see!

t_ch: 第一题：其实世界上根本没有幻造种，都是阿哈穿着玩偶头套扮演的。是真，还是假？
t_en: Question 1: Actually, imagenae don't exist at all. They're all just Aha in disguise, wearing mascot heads. True or false?

t_ch: 那阿哈还挺辛苦的。
t_en: Must be a lot of work for Aha, then.

t_ch: 假使阿哈真的任劳任怨几千年，那祂就不是欢愉星神，而是劳模星神了。
t_en: If Aha really worked THEIR fingers to the bone for thousands of years, THEY would be the Aeon of Diligence instead of the Aeon of Elation.

t_ch: 我的天，细思极恐！
t_en: Goodness, the more you think about it, the creepier it gets!

t_ch: 仔细想想，阿哈和幻造种从来没有同时出现过，阿哈也从没有否认过自己是幻造种。
t_en: Come to think of it, Aha and the imagenae have never appeared at the same time, and Aha has never denied being an imagenae.

t_ch: 下次遇见幻造种，请给它们多一些尊重。
t_en: Please show the imagenae more respect the next time you meet one.

t_ch: 阿门…怎么会说话？
t_en: A-Door... talks?

t_ch: 而且声音竟然还挺有磁性的。
t_en: With a surprisingly husky voice, too.

t_ch: 阿门都很聪明，而有些阿门格外聪明。
t_en: All A-Doors are smart, but some A-Doors are smarter than others.

t_ch: 请做出选择——
t_en: Please make your choice!

t_ch: 一阵劈里啪啦的罐头音效响起，大概是在模拟掌声吧。
t_en: A wave of clattering tin can sound effects rings out, likely meant to simulate applause.

t_ch: 恭喜你，答对了！奖池奖金翻倍，请听下一题。
t_en: Congratulations, that is the correct answer! The rewards have doubled. Now on to the next question.

t_ch: 满是叹息声的罐头音效响起。噢，糟糕了——
t_en: You are met with a wall of sighing tin can sound effects. Uh-oh...

t_ch: 很遗憾，回答错误。你已经离正常时间线越来越远了，再答错一题，你就将被阿哈的大手丢进一个没有人爱你的时间线。
t_en: Sorry, wrong answer. You're drifting further and further from the normal timeline. One more wrong answer, and Aha's giant hand will toss you into a timeline where nobody loves you.

t_ch: 请听第二题：阿哈是寰宇所有星神中最为强大的一位。是真，还是假？
t_en: Question 2: Aha is the most powerful Aeon in the universe. True or false?

t_ch: 怎么变成了主观题？
t_en: Isn't that more of a subjective question?

t_ch: 要看从哪个角度看了。
t_en: It depends on how you look at it.

t_ch: 祂不是在扮演幻造种吗？
t_en: Aren't THEY pretending to be imagenae?

t_ch: 这题考察的不是知识，而是人情世故。
t_en: The question tests not your knowledge, but your emotional intelligence.

t_ch: 这一次，你会选择真、假，还是…「场外求助」？
t_en: This time, will you choose true, false, or... "Call a Friend"?

t_ch: 不必，我有信心。
t_en: No need, I've got this.

t_ch: 怎么还有场外求助？
t_en: Since when did we have a "Call a Friend" option?

t_ch: 你有这个选择，但很抱歉，在这个异度空间里，你是联系不上其他人的。如果你不介意，我们可以给你些实用的建议。
t_en: You do have the option, but unfortunately, you won't be able to contact anyone else in this dimension. If you'd like, we can offer you some practical advice.

t_ch: 在我看来，是假的。
t_en: If you ask me, it's false.

t_ch: 依我之见，是真的。
t_en: In my opinion, it's true.

t_ch: 希望这对你有所帮助。
t_en: Hope this helps.

t_ch: 对不起，毫无帮助。
t_en: Excuse you, but that's not helpful at all.

t_ch: 谢谢，很实用！
t_en: Thanks, that's super practical!

t_ch: 请做出你的选择——
t_en: Please make your choice!

t_ch: 你尝试推理…问题的关键不在于阿哈是不是最强，而在于，祂是个谦虚的星神吗？
t_en: You try to deduce... The essence of the question isn't about whether Aha is the strongest, but rather, are THEY a humble Aeon?

t_ch: 你心中隐隐有答案，但你担心这个答案在阿哈的算计之中。你还担心你的担心也在阿哈的算计之中。
t_en: Deep down, you have an answer, but you worry it might be a part of Aha's calculations. You also worry that your worry is a part of Aha's calculations, too.

t_ch: 没办法，只能赌一把了。又或者，你也可以转头离开…如果你愿意在这条时间线继续生活的话。
t_en: There's no choice but to gamble here. Alternatively, you could just walk away... if you're content with living in this timeline.

t_ch: 喔，看你玩得还挺开心的，我是不是来得不是时候？
t_en: Oh, you seem to be having quite some fun there. Am I interrupting?

t_ch: 我正要赢得所有奖金！
t_en: I was just about to win all the prize money!

t_ch: 它们要是真的会乖乖给你奖金，就不会被我关在这里了。
t_en: If they were actually going to give you your money, I wouldn't have locked them up here.

t_ch: 是你吗，我的场外援助？
t_en: Are you my "Call a Friend"?

t_ch: 是的，以免你被它们骗到一条没有人爱你的时间线。
t_en: Yeah, so you don't get tricked into a timeline where nobody loves you.

t_ch: 不过人和两扇传送门玩得不亦乐乎，在宇宙里也不是第一次发生了。
t_en: But humans having a blast with two teleporters isn't exactly a first in the universe.

t_ch: 这两扇满嘴谎话的门不知什么时候出现在了空间站上，我就统统都当藏品笑纳了。也许我真的应该上个锁。
t_en: I don't know when these two liars appeared on the space station, so I graciously arranged accommodation for them among my collection. Maybe I should add a lock this time.

t_ch: 捞完人我也该撤了，你有空与其和它们掰扯，不如来测测模拟宇宙。等你！
t_en: Now that I've fished you out, I should get going. Instead of wasting time on them, you're better off testing the Simulated Universe. I'll be waiting!

t_ch: 很遗憾，回答错误！去没有人爱你的时间线好好生活吧。
t_en: Wrong answer, sorry! Off you go to a timeline where nobody loves you.

t_ch: 最后给你个忠告，朋友。不要相信门，更不要相信会说话的门。
t_en: One last piece of advice, friend. Don't trust doors, and especially doors that talk.

t_ch: 啊啊啊啊——
t_en: Aaaah!

t_ch: 你感到自己在向下坠落，一阵眩晕感抓住了你…但与此同时，另一只手抓住了你。
t_en: You feel yourself falling, a wave of dizziness grabs hold of you... but in the same moment, another hand seizes you.

t_ch: 我这是到哪儿来了？
t_en: Where am I?

t_ch: 你从哪来的，现在就在哪里。
t_en: You're right where you came from.

t_ch: 面前有两扇门，一扇是阿门，另一扇也是阿门。
t_en: There are two doors in front of you. An A-Door, and another A-Door.

t_ch: 推门回去。
t_en: Push open the door and go back.

t_ch: 转身离开。
t_en: Turn around and leave.

t_ch: （刚才是我看错了吗？啵啵娃老师应该是在这的…吧？）
t_en: (Wait, am I seeing things? Ms. Dollie should be here... right?)

t_ch: （找个机会问问她吧。）
t_en: (I should find a chance to ask her about it.)

t_ch: 老师，我在走廊上见到你了。
t_en: I saw you in the hallway, teacher.

t_ch: 呵，亲眼所见，就是真实吗？
t_en: Heh, so seeing is believing, is it?

t_ch: 什么意思，新的谜语吗？
t_en: What's that supposed to mean? Some kind of new riddle?

t_ch: 「耳听为虚，眼见为实」这不过是在两种最常见的幻觉里选择了一种罢了。
t_en: "Hearing is deceptive, seeing is believing." That's just choosing one of the two most common illusions.

t_ch: 我看见你还故意等了我！
t_en: I saw you deliberately waiting for me!

t_ch: 那么你和那个「啵啵娃老师」说上话了吗？你真的能确定那就是我吗？
t_en: So did you manage to talk to that "Ms. Dollie?" Are you really sure that was me?

t_ch: …我知道你有很多疑惑。不妨，听我讲个故事吧。
t_en: ...I know you have a lot of questions. Why don't I tell you a story?

t_ch: 其实在我第一次进行星际旅行的时候，我的老师曾经如此告诉我「啵啵娃，你是否相信世界上有着两片一模一样的叶子？」
t_en: Actually, when I first embarked on interstellar travel, my teacher once asked me, "Dollie, do you believe there are two exactly identical leaves in this world?"

t_ch: 在第一次在穿越黑洞时，我见识到了多重平行宇宙的可能性。
t_en: The first time I crossed through a black hole, I caught a glimpse of the possibility of multiple parallel universes.

t_ch: 时间的流动并非是线性的，我曾见到过另一个与我一模一样的存在——她的眼神温柔又哀伤，似乎她经历了一切却不愿意向我透露半个字。
t_en: Time doesn't flow in a straight line. I once encountered another version of myself, identical in every way. Her eyes were tender yet sorrowful, as if she'd been through it all, but she refused to breathe a word of it to me.

t_ch: …我很久没有见到她了，我确信她已经做出了某种牺牲。
t_en: ...I haven't seen her in ages. I'm sure she's made some kind of sacrifice.

t_ch: 任谁都会这样想吧——世界上还有另一个我，替我默默承受了世间的一切意外与不幸，才能保佑无知且幸福的我顺利地活到了现在。
t_en: Anyone would think the same, right? That somewhere out there, there's another me, silently bearing all the world's accidents and misfortunes on my behalf, keeping me blissfully ignorant and safe and sound all the time.

t_ch: …我应当对她感激，但是她会抱怨命运吗？
t_en: ...I should be grateful to her, but would she complain about destiny?

t_ch: 我还活着，她已经死去，她会接受这样的安排吗？
t_en: I'm still alive, and she's already passed on. Would she really be okay with that?

t_ch: 她会吧，毕竟这就是她的命运。
t_en: She will. After all, it's her destiny.

t_ch: 她不会，没人应该为他人承担。
t_en: She won't. No one should have to shoulder another person's burdens.

t_ch: 你在绘世学院的走廊上见到一位与我长得很像的「啵啵娃老师」，她不与你打招呼，却在等着你追上她的脚步，她似乎有意将你引向什么地方……
t_en: You spotted a "Ms. Dollie" in the hallways of Graphia Academy who looks a lot like me. She didn't greet you, but she did wait for you to catch up... It seems like she was trying to lead you somewhere...

t_ch: 你现在明白了，命运选择了让她再次出现，你见到的那个「啵啵娃老师」其实……
t_en: You understand now. Destiny chose to make her reappear. The "Ms. Dollie" that you met was actually...

t_ch: 诶，其实就是我。
t_en: Heh, it was actually me.

t_ch: 那…世界上另一个啵啵娃呢？
t_en: So... what about the other Dollie in the world?

t_ch: 这是我刚才编的。
t_en: I just made that up.

t_ch: 那…你讲这么多是为了什么？
t_en: So... what was the point of all this?

t_ch: 为了好玩。
t_en: For giggles.

t_ch: 现在真相大白了，哈哈。下课了，你去休息吧。
t_en: Now the truth's all out in the open, haha. Class dismissed, go get some rest.

t_ch: （创世涡心…也变成了这样。）
t_en: (The Vortex of Genesis... has turned out like this too.)

t_ch: （去看看水盆的情况吧。）
t_en: (Better go check the basin.)

t_ch: 宝贝儿…仙馔秘酿…灌进来…我给你馈赠……
t_en: My dear... Celestial Ambrosia... Pour some in... I'll give you a present...

t_ch: 你还好吗？
t_en: Are you alright?

t_ch: 不好，还是好没意思…黑漆漆的…又小又窄，难受……
t_en: No... it's so boring here... dark and cramped and small... it feels awful...

t_ch: 醒醒，你变蓝啦。
t_en: Wake up... you've turned blue.

t_ch: 嗯？蓝色…我是大海？没有啊？呵呵…你也…被秘酿灌倒了…嗯？
t_en: Hmm? Blue... am I the ocean? Nah, hehe... you got drunk on ambrosia too... huh?

t_ch: 不说这个，宝贝儿…带秘酿来…珍宝、圣物…随你挑……
t_en: Never mind that. My dear... Bring ambrosia here... Treasures, relics, beauties... Take your pick...

t_ch: （它意识不到周围的变化吗？还是说…只是醉过头了？）
t_en: (Can't it notice the changes around? Or is it simply... too intoxicated?)

t_ch: …尚未发现秘酿能够完全颠覆个体对外界环境认知的案例。猜想：无名客曾与该个体接触，或有可能影响该数据体认知功能……
t_en: ...No documented cases of ambrosia completely overriding an individual's perception of external reality. Conjecture: The Nameless previously interacted with this entity, potentially affecting its cognitive functions...

t_ch: （这是…来古士观察它时的记忆？）
t_en: (Is this... Lygus's observation memory?)

t_ch: 秘酿…给我秘酿…我准备了更多的宝贝…为了你…宝贝儿……
t_en: Ambrosia... bring me the ambrosia... I've got more treasures... for you... darling...

t_ch: 今天给你带了点不一样的。
t_en: I brought something special today.

t_ch: 小宝贝儿…带秘酿来了吗？快、快！想喝新鲜的了……
t_en: My dear... Did you bring me ambrosia? Hurry! I want a fresh drink...

t_ch: 聆听星宿之声。
t_en: Listen to the Voice of Constellation.

t_ch: 进入潮汐的馈赠。
t_en: Enter Tidal Bounty.

t_ch: 跟这水盆聊聊。
t_en: Chat with this basin.

t_ch: 聊天…不喝点儿…没法聊……
t_en: Chat... I can't... without something to drink...

t_ch: 你是什么东西？
t_en: What are you?

t_ch: 我是…我是什么呢？
t_en: I am... What am I?

t_ch: 对了，我是个醒不来的醉客…不，我是个风流成性的舞者…不、不不，我是个耽迷纵欲的君王……
t_en: Oh yes, I am a drunk that can't sober up... No, I am a dancer frolicking among my loves... No, I am a king indulgent in desires of flesh...

t_ch: 并非自相矛盾的胡言乱语。推测：此一系列定义全部都是「某一时刻」发生过的「事实」……
t_en: Not incoherent ramblings. Speculation: This sequence of definitions reflects "facts" from "discrete temporal instances"...

t_ch: …果然，以上定义指向{RUBY_B#第<unbreak>287</unbreak>次「再创世」}实验#287{RUBY_E#}、{RUBY_B#第<unbreak>8745</unbreak>次「再创世」}实验#8745{RUBY_E#}及{RUBY_B#第<unbreak>134962</unbreak>次「再创世」}实验#134962{RUBY_E#}中标签为「海洋」的黄金裔模型。推测成立。
t_en: ...Confirmed. These definitions correlate with the "Ocean" variant Chrysos Heir models in the {RUBY_B#287th Era Nova}Experiment #287{RUBY_E#}, {RUBY_B#8745th Era Nova}Experiment #8745{RUBY_E#}, and {RUBY_B#134,<unbreak>962</unbreak>nd Era Nova}Experiment #134,<unbreak>962</unbreak>{RUBY_E#}. Speculation substantiated.

t_ch: 跟我聊聊秘酿。
t_en: Talk to me about ambrosia.

t_ch: 秘酿…仙馔秘酿是至宝！用大地的身体发酵…灌进天空的云朵里…他生气了，嘻嘻嘻……
t_en: Ambrosia... Celestial Ambrosia is supreme! Fermented with the land's body... encased in the sky's clouds... He's angry, heeheehee...

t_ch: 能够与模型神经网络结合，使其认知决策逻辑产生异常噪声，借用熟知的概念——
t_en: It's capable of integrating with the model neural network, introducing disruptive noise into its cognitive decision-making logic. This aligns with the known concept —

t_ch: 「对称占用式逻辑编码」，简称「死锁」。
t_en: "Symmetrical Occupancy Logic Encoding," which is commonly termed, the "Deadlock."

t_ch: 跟我说说这个地方吧。
t_en: Tell me about this place.

t_ch: 我告诉你，宝贝儿…过去这儿可不是这样的…鱼儿们…起舞…醉客们…纵欲、起舞、纵欲……
t_en: Let me tell you, my dear... It wasn't like this in the past... The fish... they dance... The drunks... frolicking, dancing, frolicking...

t_ch: 有趣。如果无名客得知「它的地理位置位于斯缇科西亚正下方」这一信息，他们又将对实验产生何种影响？
t_en: Fascinating. How might the Nameless influence the experiment if made aware of "its geospatial position is directly beneath the Styxia"?

t_ch: 烦了，不聊了吧。
t_en: Enough. That's it for today.

t_ch: 宝贝儿…带秘酿来…不管聊天还是别的…我都陪你……
t_en: My dear... Bring ambrosia... Chat or otherwise... I'll accompany you...

t_ch: 离开
t_en: Leave

t_ch: 下次…记得带秘酿来…多带点……
t_en: Next time... remember to bring ambrosia... More...

t_ch: 社长大人，你关注二相乐园的热搜趋势榜了吗？现在网友们到处都在议论：火花大会上到底发生了什么？球棒浣熊是怎么打赢的？
t_en: Boss, have you been keeping up with Planarcadia's trending news? Everyone's talking about it: What exactly went down at SparxiCon? How did Baseball Raccoon manage to win?

t_ch: 我灵敏的小耳朵已经动哩！《狸狸周刊》绝对不能错过这些新鲜出炉、冒着热气的愿力，是时候开一场新闻发布会，为咱们杂志社吸引一波关注度了。
t_en: My keen little ears are twitching! Furbobo Weekly absolutely cannot miss out on this piping hot, fresh-off-the-press Wishpower. Time to hold a press conference and drum up some buzz for our magazine!

t_ch: 走吧，迫不及待亮相了。
t_en: Let's go. I can't wait to make my entrance!

t_ch: 收到收到，狸刻行动。
t_en: Roger that, furbo on the move!

t_ch: 发布？发布什么？
t_en: A press release? About what?

t_ch: 观众爱看什么，我们就发布什么。问题已经准备好了，咱相信社长大人的网感哩！
t_en: We post what the readers want to see. Questions locked and loaded. We trust your internet instincts, boss!

t_ch: 噢对了，趁着我的摄像机还没有开启录制，咱们先给杂志社进行一个升级，装点装点门面吧！要是拍到乱糟糟的画面，不利于《狸狸周刊》的形象展示哩！
t_en: Oh, right! Before I start recording, let's give the magazine office a level-up and spruce up the place! Can't have a messy backdrop on camera, that wouldn't do Furbobo Weekly's image any favors!

t_ch: 一键升级！
t_en: One-click level up!

t_ch: 完美，我已经拍下来作为发布会对外宣传的第一个镜头了。
t_en: Perfect! I've already snapped the first shot. It'll be the opening image for the press release.

t_ch: 接下来——闪现发布会现场！咱们去绘世学院。
t_en: Next... straight to the venue! We're headed to Graphia Academy.

t_ch: 嘉宾已到场，有请浣熊面具谒者、《苍天航路绒绒号》主角原型、火花大会的终结者、出道两年半未尝败绩的——{NICKNAME}接受采访！
t_en: Our guest has arrived! Please welcome the Raccoon-Masked Supplicant, the real-life inspiration behind Fluffy Across the Blue, the one who ended SparxiCon, {F#she}{M#he} who is undefeated for two and a half years since debut — {NICKNAME} for the interview!

t_ch: 啊哩！这么多人？何时来的？
t_en: Whoa! Where did all these people come from? When did you all get here?

t_ch: 大家好，我是满愿电视台新闻栏目《乐园早知道》的记者，咕咕呱！
t_en: Hi everyone, I'm a reporter from Fulwish TV's news program Arcadian Morning Report, Bubble Beak!

t_ch: 观众朋友们好，小女子乃是仙舟「玉阙」使团随行来客，身兼仙舟《银河探索》编辑部特别驻外记者，名唤停云。
t_en: Greetings, dear viewers! I am a guest accompanying the Xianzhou Yuque delegation, and I also serve as a special foreign correspondent for the Cosmic Exploration editorial department. My name is Tingyun.

t_ch: 不愧是热搜榜上买房的{NICKNAME}，咱们的现场居然来了这么多记者。现在，由{NICKNAME}自由选择接受哪位记者的采访。
t_en: As expected from {NICKNAME}, our resident camper on the trending board! We've got quite the press turnout here. Now, {NICKNAME} gets to pick which reporter to take questions from.

t_ch: 前排左手边第一位。
t_en: Front row, first from the left.

t_ch: 不是噗噗，不是雪雪，请记住我的名字，来自满愿电视台的记者咕、咕、呱！
t_en: I'm neither Poofy nor Snowie, please remember my name! I'm Fulwish TV's reporter: Bubble Beak!

t_ch: 首先恭喜{NICKNAME}击败了火花，这是我从业以来，第一次看到一个既不是二相乐园原住民也没有公司编制的外地人…啊哦，一个外来谒者获胜。呵呵，还是蛮稀奇的。
t_en: First off, congratulations to {NICKNAME} for defeating Sparxie. In all my years in this business, this is the first time I've seen an outsider who's neither a Planarcadia native nor on the IPC payro— 'scuse me, I mean a Supplicant from outside — take the win. Oh-ho, quite the rare sight.

t_ch: 呃，谢谢。
t_en: Uh, thanks.

t_ch: 注意你的提问措辞。
t_en: Watch your tone.

t_ch: 啊哦！有什么不妥吗？要做二相乐园最严厉的记者，正是我的采访风格。
t_en: Oh-ho! Something wrong? As someone who wants aspires to be Planarcadia's toughest reporter, that's just my interviewing style.

t_ch: 正式开启第一个问题吧！我观察到，{NICKNAME}在绘世学院的幻境中度过了两年的学生生涯，也就是说，花了整整「两年」时间才识破火花的幻境……
t_en: Let's officially begin with Question 1. In my observation, {NICKNAME} spent two full years as a student inside Graphia Academy's illusion. In other words, it took you two whole years to see through Sparxie's illusion...

t_ch: …有点慢说实话。当时是怎么想的？是以*安全*为首要考虑吗？
t_en: ...a bit slow, to be honest. What were you thinking back then? Was safety your top priority?

t_ch: 你对我有错误认知。
t_en: You've got the wrong read on me.

t_ch: 你仔细想想，火花花了两年也没给我洗脑成功，到底谁更夯谁更拉？
t_en: Think about it: Sparxicle spent two years trying to brainwash me without success. In the end, who truly holds the power, and who is simply outclassed?

t_ch: 啊哦！好吧，有几分道理。
t_en: Oh-ho! Fair point.

t_ch: 我怕伤到无辜的学生。
t_en: I'm concerned about innocent students being hurt.

t_ch: 学校是打架的地方吗？我只是留了一手，不到万不得已，不打算动手。
t_en: Is the school a place to fight? I was holding back. I wasn't going to go on offensive unless I absolutely had to.

t_ch: 真的吗？在学校里打架的事，无名客们也不是没干过吧？我可是看过《苍天航路绒绒号》毗乃昆尼篇的……
t_en: Really? It's not like the Nameless haven't gotten into scraps at school before. I've totally watched the Pinecany arc of Fluffy Across the Blue...

t_ch: 你以为我不想快点吗？
t_en: You think I didn't want to hurry it up?

t_ch: 你以为我不想快点吗，这件事很难的，换你的话，没有十年做不到好吧！
t_en: You think I didn't want to hurry it up? This is an immense undertaking. If you were in my position, it would take you a decade to achieve what I have.

t_ch: 你看你看，早干嘛了，怎么现在开始急了？
t_en: Oh look what we have here? What did you think was going to happen? Why are you getting defensive?

t_ch: 现在进入第二个问题：《乐园早知道》栏目全程播放了火花大会的现场实况，我也看了，对于你的首战表现，我的评价是：还不错。
t_en: Now for Question 2. Arcadian Morning Report aired the entire SparxiCon event live. I watched it. My assessment of your debut performance is: not bad.

t_ch: 所以，{NICKNAME}考虑之后接受满愿电视台的赞助吗？
t_en: So, {NICKNAME}, have you considered taking Fulwish TV's sponsorship?

t_ch: （询问赞助的用意）
t_en: (Ask about the intention of the sponsorship)

t_ch: 老实说，你们只是馋我带来的收视率吧？
t_en: Be honest: You just want the ratings I bring, don't you?

t_ch: 呵呵，这就是商业电视台的运作逻辑，但你也能借助满愿电视台获得更高的关注度，怎么想都不亏吧。
t_en: Hehe. That's how commercial TV works. But you'll get way more exposure through Fulwish TV, too. However you slice it, you're not losing out.

t_ch: （质疑对方的态度）
t_en: (Question their attitude)

t_ch: 对我的评价只有「还不错」？就凭你的态度……
t_en: Just "not bad"? With that attitude of yours...

t_ch: 呵呵，我只是个合同工，爱怎么评论就怎么评论。我不对满愿电视台的业绩负责，领导要是看我不爽可以开除我。
t_en: Heh. I'm just a contract staff. I'll say what I want. I'm not responsible for Fulwish TV's performance. If the higher-ups don't like it, they can fire me.

t_ch: （看看电视台开价多少）
t_en: (See how much the TV station is offering)

t_ch: 你们有多少诚意？我要看看满愿电视台能给多少赞助费。
t_en: How serious are you guys? I want to see how much sponsorship fee Fulwish TV is willing to offer.

t_ch: …啊哦！你是外来客可能不知道，满愿电视台是二相乐园最大的电视台，愿力要多少有多少。当然，前提是你有足够的商业价值……
t_en: ...Oh! You're an outsider, so you might not know this. Fulwish TV is the biggest network in Planarcadia. They've got Wishpower coming out of their ears. Of course, that's only if you're worth the price...

t_ch: 好好考虑吧，{NICKNAME}，错过这个机会可就没有了。
t_en: Think it over carefully, {NICKNAME}. Miss this chance and it's gone for good.

t_ch: 你的提问机会也结束了，满愿电视台的采访就到这里！
t_en: And your question time's up too. That's all for Fulwish TV's interview!

t_ch: 前排左手边第二位。
t_en: Front row, second from the left.

t_ch: 哔哔哔，到我了到我了，《狸狸周刊》的特派记者幻太子来了啵！
t_en: Beep-beep, my turn, my turn! Paraprince, special correspondent for Furbobo Weekly, reporting in, boop!

t_ch: 我一直潜伏在火花大会现场，辛辛苦苦地跟拍素材，还被火花「校长」抓住现形送了我一发大点赞，{NICKNAME}是不是该奖励我回杂志社啵？
t_en: I've been deep undercover at SparxiCon, working tirelessly to capture exclusive footage. I even had a run-in with "Dean" Sparxie, who caught me in the act and gave me a big old "Like" for my troubles. Surely that's worth a special commendation back at the editorial department, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 外派绘世学院不好吗？
t_en: What's wrong with being stationed at Graphia Academy?

t_ch: 可以，再奖励你多问几个问题。
t_en: Sure thing. As a reward, I'll let you ask a few more questions.

t_ch: 好的啵！好的啵！这就来提问。
t_en: Yay-boop! Here comes the questions.

t_ch: 第一个问题：乐园热心群众目睹列车组的丹恒先生和一名神秘男子曾出现在发生血案的鸽川区，还有传言认为，星穹列车不止有{NICKNAME}持有的一副面具……
t_en: First question: Eyewitnesses in the park spotted Mr. Dan Heng of the Astral Express and a mysterious man in Dovebrook District, the site of a recent homicide. Furthermore, rumors suggest that {NICKNAME} isn't the only member of the Express in possession of a mask.

t_ch: 在「火花大会」事件中，{NICKNAME}与人联手对战火花，有人表示星穹列车不讲武德！
t_en: During the SparxiCon incident, you, {NICKNAME}, teamed up with others against Sparxie, leading some to claim the Astral Express doesn't play fair.

t_ch: 无论是承认还是澄清，请问{NICKNAME}能否披露更多细节呢？
t_en: Whether you're here to confirm or clarify, could you disclose more details on these matters?

t_ch: 也没说不能这么打吧？
t_en: Since when was that against the rules?

t_ch: 幻月游戏的竞争规则里没有必须一对一的说法吧？这么多火花模因病毒感染者打我一个人，才是不讲武德。
t_en: Nothing in the Phantasmoon Games' rules of engagement don't stipulate a one-on-one duel. If anything, having a horde of Sparxie-meme-infected hostiles swarming a single person is what I'd call not playing fair.

t_ch: 再退一万步说，我单枪匹马也不是无法战胜她嘛，对吧？
t_en: Besides, even if I were to single-handedly take her on, I'd still come out on top. Wouldn't I?

t_ch: 有道狸！得以压制住火花病毒的扩散为优先考虑啵！
t_en: You're absolutely right! Containing the Sparxie Virus spread comes first, boop!

t_ch: 我无可奉告，这是绝密级消息。
t_en: No comment. That's classified information.

t_ch: 哪有上来就掀人老底的？有关面具的情报我还不能透露，你自己想办法打听去吧。
t_en: Who opens by digging up someone's dirt like that? I'm not at liberty to disclose anything about the masks. If you want the scoop, you'll have to do your own legwork.

t_ch: 哦哦哦？这是对我的考验吗？请放心，像我这样的小区做题家狸猫，绝对会用尽全力啵！
t_en: Ohhh? Is this a challenge? Don't worry. I'm the kind of neighborhood quiz-grinder furbo who'll give it everything, boop!

t_ch: 你到底是哪边的记者？
t_en: Which side are you reporting for, exactly?

t_ch: 你到底是《狸狸周刊》的记者还是对家来的卧底啊？
t_en: Are you Furbobo Weekly's reporter, or a mole from the competition?

t_ch: 对不起！咱们《狸狸周刊》就是从八卦小报发家的啵！忘情了发狠了连社长都逃不过被八卦啵！
t_en: Sorry! Furbobo Weekly started as a gossip rag, after all! Once we get rolling, we don't stop, we'd even dig up dirt on our own boss if the scoop was good enough, boop!

t_ch: 那么，第二个问题……
t_en: Alright, second question...

t_ch: 好多人都留意到，决战现场出现了一个红色的火花「旧型号」，但是火花大会落幕后，她们一起消失不见了啵！
t_en: There have been many reports of an old red Sparxie model at the decisive battle. But after SparxiCon wrapped up, they vanished together without a trace, boop!

t_ch: 到底谁是真正的火花呢？如果一定要选，{NICKNAME}是更喜欢火花现在的直播形象，还是她的「旧型号」呢？我已经纠结半天了，根本选不出来哔啵！
t_en: So who's the real Sparxie? If you had to pick, {NICKNAME}, do you prefer Sparxie's current streaming persona, or her "old model"? I've been agonizing over this forever, and I just can't decide, beep-boop!

t_ch: 这个……
t_en: Well...

t_ch: 狸了个大谱！你的问题怎么全是火花？果然，你平时没少看火花的直播吧？
t_en: Holy furboly! Why are all your questions about Sparxie? I knew it! You watch Sparxie's streams all the time, don't you?

t_ch: 交出私人账号，没收。
t_en: Hand over your personal account. Confiscated.

t_ch: 啊…如果真的要没收，请让我删除一些收藏夹啵……
t_en: Ah... If you really have to confiscate it, at least let me delete a few bookmarks first, boop...

t_ch: 杂志社是工作，火花是生活？
t_en: The magazine is work, and Sparxie is life?

t_ch: 我爱工作，也爱火花，我热爱的都是我的生活啵……
t_en: I love work, and I love Sparxie. Everything I love is my life, boop...

t_ch: 哔哔哔，危险危险危险。换个问题好了…啊！问这个吧！
t_en: Beepbeepbeep, danger alert. Switching questions... Ah! Let's try this one!

t_ch: 其实，我确实在火花粉丝群里潜伏了很久，发现粉丝们对火花吃瘪一事大为光火，扬言要「花粉出征、寸草不生」，翻出你的所有网络账号啵！
t_en: Actually, I've been lurking in Sparxhead groups for quite a while, and I discovered the fans were absolutely furious about Sparxie getting owned. They're threatening to "mobilize the Sparxheads and leave nothing standing," vowing to dig up all your online accounts!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}准备怎么应对这些狂热的粉丝呢？
t_en: How do you, {NICKNAME}, plan to handle these rabid fans?

t_ch: 不如线下真人快打。
t_en: How about settling it face-to-face?

t_ch: 是吗？别光用键盘说，跟我的球棒说去吧。
t_en: Oh yeah? Don't just run your mouth behind a screen. Talk to my bat.

t_ch: 浣熊大人的球棒，恐怕比火花大人的点赞锤子还难以消受啵！
t_en: The Great Raccoon's bat is probably even harder to handle than Sparxie's Like Hammer, boop!

t_ch: 这些人真的是粉丝吗？
t_en: Are those people even fans?

t_ch: 不能接受自己喜欢的偶像落败的人…我觉得他们都是假粉诶！作为一只聪明的小狸猫，你可千万不要被这种自称是「粉丝」的人骗了啊~
t_en: People who can't handle their idol losing? Fake fans, for sure! You're a smart little furbo, you definitely shouldn't get fooled by people who call themselves "fans" like that!

t_ch: 原来如此，学吧，太深了！
t_en: I see! That's a deep lesson for us all!

t_ch: 我要上告给执行官。
t_en: I'm reporting this to the CEO.

t_ch: 上告给执行官！我要告到首席执行官真珠那里。
t_en: I'm escalating this to the C-suite! I'll see to it that CEO Pearl hears about this personally.

t_ch: 直通首席执行官，还是浣熊大人有门路啵！
t_en: A direct line to the CEO... the Great Raccoon really has connections, boop!

t_ch: 我的账号也不是什么秘密吧？
t_en: It's not like my account's a secret, right?

t_ch: 随便翻，我在网上也叫{NICKNAME}，请认准全网唯一账号。
t_en: Go ahead and dig. All my socials are {NICKNAME}, too. It's my one and only account across the whole web.

t_ch: 我有预感，你要涨粉了啵……
t_en: I've got a feeling... you're about to blow up, boop...

t_ch: 时间差不多哩，《狸狸周刊》的采访到此结束。
t_en: Welp, that's about all the time we have! This Furbobo Weekly interview is officially wrapped.

t_ch: 前排左手边第三位。
t_en: Front row, third from the left.

t_ch: 二相乐园的朋友们好，恩公好~小女子停云，是仙舟《银河探索》编辑部派来二相乐园的采访记者。
t_en: Greetings, friends of Planarcadia, and greetings to you, benefactor! I am Tingyun, a reporter from the Cosmic Exploration editorial department of the Xianzhou, here in Planarcadia to conduct interviews.

t_ch: 他乡遇故知，好亲切。
t_en: Running into a familiar face so far from home, what a pleasant surprise!

t_ch: 是呀，瞧见恩公分外亲切，这回我也准备了不少惊喜给恩公。
t_en: It is! It's always such a joy to see you, benefactor. And this time, I've prepared quite a few surprises for you as well.

t_ch: 《银河探索》编辑部…？
t_en: Cosmic Exploration editorial department...?

t_ch: 您瞧，仙舟「玉阙」的戎韬将军出使二相乐园，随行有一名记者，也方便整理各项外交事宜呀。
t_en: You see, the Seer Strategist of Xianzhou Yuque is on a diplomatic mission to Planarcadia. Having a reporter along also makes it easier to organize all kinds of diplomatic affairs.

t_ch: *小声*这段冒充「仙舟第一杀手」的经历，还请恩公帮我多掩盖一段时间。
t_en: *whisper* Please help me keep my little stint as "Xianzhou's Number One Assassin" under wraps for a while longer, benefactor.

t_ch: 先祝贺{NICKNAME}在火花大会旗开得胜，仙舟的父老乡亲们也很关注{NICKNAME}的二相乐园之旅，想拜托小女子借此机会，替他们说上几句。
t_en: First, congratulations on your opening victory at SparxiCon, {NICKNAME}. Folks back home on the Xianzhou have been following your Planarcadia journey closely, and asked me to pass along a few words on their behalf.

t_ch: 但我想，转述不如一见。不妨咱们现场连线，这算不算最大的惊喜？
t_en: But I think words can't beat a reunion. Shall we patch them through live? Wouldn't that be the biggest surprise?

t_ch: 哇！{NICKNAME}，真的是{NICKNAME}，我想死你了！
t_en: Whoa! {NICKNAME}, it's really {NICKNAME}! I've missed you SO much!

t_ch: 小桂子我有个问题想问你，听说二相乐园人多鬼多，适合直播，{NICKNAME}觉得小桂子和裳裳的捉鬼直播开到这里来有市场吗？
t_en: Little Gui's got a question for ya: I heard Planarcadia is packed with people and ghosts, perfect for streaming. {NICKNAME}, do you think Little Gui and Shangshang's ghost-hunting livestream would have a market if we brought it over?

t_ch: 要想赚钱，得先花钱。
t_en: You gotta spend money to make money.

t_ch: 这里流行的是虚拟主播，我觉得…要想赚到直播间的流量，得先在「乐画师」网站上找绘师约一个虚拟主播形象。
t_en: VStreamers are all the rage here. I think... if you want to rake in the clout, you'd need to commission an artist on the Joy Painter website first to design your VStreamer persona.

t_ch: 「乐画师」？我记下了，你可别骗我啊。
t_en: Joy Painter? Got it, memorized! You better not be pulling my leg here.

t_ch: 太有了！这里到处是幽灵。
t_en: Absolutely! This place is crawling with ghosts.

t_ch: 虽然和绥园的幽灵不是一个品种，但这里的灵异事件可不少，什么放到厕所顶部的卷纸啦、会自动写字的黑板啦，啊——看你背后……
t_en: Not the same kind as Fyxestroll Garden, but the paranormal stuff here is nonstop. Toilet paper rolls balanced on top of stalls, blackboards that write by themselves, and— Ahhhh look behind you...

t_ch: 哇啊，什么都没有啊！
t_en: Waaah! There's nothing there!

t_ch: 哼，这招吓裳裳有用，吓我就算啦！
t_en: Hmph, that trick might work on Shangshang, but it won't scare me!

t_ch: 我帮小桂子推流，收入分成。
t_en: I'll help Little Gui boost the stream, and we split the profits.

t_ch: 我在二相乐园有了一间杂志社，能发动狸脉帮你的账号推流，收入你三我七，够义气吧？
t_en: I've got a magazine office in Planarcadia. I can rally my furbo network to push your account. You get thirty, I take seventy. Pretty generous, right?

t_ch: 够…够奸诈的！
t_en: Th—That's robbery!

t_ch: 小桂子的问题结束了，下一位是……
t_en: Little Gui's question is done. Next is...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，好久不见。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, long time no see.

t_ch: 听说与仙舟演武比试不同，二相乐园的比试以是否足够「欢愉」作为条件，彦卿有点难以想象，想请教{NICKNAME}的战术思路是什么？
t_en: I've heard Planarcadia's matches aren't like Xianzhou sparring. They're judged on whether they've enough "Elation." Yanqing can't quite picture it. Could you share your tactical thinking, {NICKNAME}?

t_ch: 我当二相乐园达人秀演。
t_en: I thought this was Planarcadia's Got Talent.

t_ch: 演就完事了，正经备战就不好玩了。
t_en: Just wing it. Where's the fun if we take prep seriously?

t_ch: 拿从前的经历打个比方，你对着观众大喊一声「我叫冷锋」，比拿起剑认真比试要有胜算多了，懂了吗？
t_en: Let me put it this way, if you were to shout "My name is Lengfeng!" at the audience, you'd have a way better shot at winning than actually picking up a sword and dueling seriously. Get what I'm saying?

t_ch: 我懂了吗？我好像懂了。
t_en: Do I get it... I think I do.

t_ch: 我做好随时跑路的准备。
t_en: I'm ready to bail at a moment's notice.

t_ch: 笑死，根本没有对策。不光是对手，连我自己也猜不到下一步我要干什么。
t_en: This is too funny. There's no plan. Not even my opponent can guess my next move. Heck, even I don't know what I'm doing next!

t_ch: 但仙舟有句古话说得好：乱拳打死老师傅。这就是我的对策。
t_en: But there's an old Xianzhou saying: Even flailing fists can take down a master. That's my strategy.

t_ch: 这…想必那位常乐天君反倒会为此开怀吧。
t_en: Well... I imagine that Joyseeker would actually be delighted by this turn of events.

t_ch: 你来参加，我亲自告诉你。
t_en: Show up, and I'll tell you myself.

t_ch: 少年，你要学的还有很多，来参加幻月游戏吧，我亲自和你在够不够「欢愉」上过两招。
t_en: You've got a lot to learn, kid. Join the Phantasmoon Games. I'll personally show you what true "Elation" looks like.

t_ch: 好危险，我还是老实练剑吧。
t_en: Too dangerous. I'll stick to practicing my swordplay.

t_ch: 好啦！彦卿小公子的问题也结束了。恩公的回答信息满满，令人收获颇丰呐，小女子没有别的问题要问了。
t_en: Alright! That wraps up Young Master Yanqing's questions too. Benefactor, your answers were so informative and insightful. This humble lady has no further questions to ask.

t_ch: 背景墙上那位。
t_en: That person on the wall over there.

t_ch: 被你发现了，我不止是绘世学院的教导主任，也是《学园之声》栏目的创始人。我的意思是说，绘世学院的校园媒体归我管。
t_en: You caught me. I'm not just the Dean of Graphia Academy, I'm also the founder of Campus Voice. In other words, all campus media at Graphia Academy falls under my purview.

t_ch: 第一次听说这个栏目。
t_en: First time hearing about this program.

t_ch: 唉，校园媒体要走出校园，还有很长的路要走。
t_en: Yeah, campus media's got a long way to go before it breaks out of the campus bubble.

t_ch: 我只是随手一指。
t_en: I just picked one at random.

t_ch: 指到就是缘分，你的确很懂我们缘生幻造种。
t_en: It's fate that you picked me. You truly understand us ideogenitive imagenae.

t_ch: 无论如何，感谢你拯救了绘世学院。我就代表《学园之声》找几个问题问问你吧。
t_en: Either way, thank you for saving Graphia Academy. Let me ask a few questions on behalf of Campus Voice.

t_ch: 嗯…第一个问题：你对绘世学院的课堂怎么看？换句话说，在这座二相乐园最富盛名的学校里，有没有哪位老师或哪门课程为你留下了深刻的印象？
t_en: Hmm... First question: What do you think of the classes at Graphia Academy? In other words, did any particular teacher or course leave a lasting impression on you at Planarcadia's most prestigious school?

t_ch: 我永远喜欢火花校长。
t_en: I'll always love Dean Sparxie.

t_ch: 火花！火花！我最喜欢校长火花！
t_en: Sparxie! Sparxie! I love Dean Sparxie the most!

t_ch: 哦不，孩子，我帮你拨打保健室电话吧，治疗模因病毒的医生在哪里？
t_en: Oh no, kid, let me call the nurse's office for you. Where's the doctor who treats memetic viruses?

t_ch: 绯英老师的绘画课。
t_en: Teacher Evanescia's painting class.

t_ch: 绯英老师的绘画艺术，真是神乎其迹，我特别喜欢她笔下的我。
t_en: Ms. Evanescia's art is absolutely divine! I especially love how she captures me on canvas.

t_ch: 飞…嘤…？是我耳背了吗？绘世学院有这位老师吗？
t_en: Efan... nesha? Am I suddenly deaf? Does Graphia Academy even have a teacher by that name?

t_ch: 海龙主任的冲浪课。
t_en: Dean Seadrake's surfing class.

t_ch: 「冲浪」是何意？如果是说《覆世沧浪图》的冲浪，年轻时我确实曾在画中尽情遨游，那番感受…我倒！现在回想起来也令人心情激荡……
t_en: Surfing? Whatever does that mean? If you mean surfing through Worldbreaker Waves... I must admit, in my greener years, I did indeed immerse myself within those painted tides. That feeling is simply... bodacious! The memory alone remains quite evocative...

t_ch: 就是这样，海龙主任能让我学习到<unbreak>20</unbreak>年前的网络冲浪梗…「我倒」！
t_en: Exactly! Dean Seadrake can teach me internet slangs from twenty years ago... "It was bodacious!"

t_ch: 我都…上了什么课来着？
t_en: What... What classes did I even take?

t_ch: 抱歉，上学这种事，总是学的越多，忘得越快。
t_en: Sorry, but that's how school works. The more you cram, the faster you forget.

t_ch: 你…哎，如此说来，是我教导主任失格了。
t_en: You... *sigh* I suppose that makes me a failure as your Dean of Discipline.

t_ch: 接下来是第二个问题，经过这次火花大会，你有什么校园安全上的建议要对绘世学院说吗？
t_en: Alright, second question: After this whole SparxiCon incident, do you have any campus safety recommendations for Graphia Academy?

t_ch: 加强校园门禁管理。
t_en: Tighten up campus security.

t_ch: 火花能混进学校里，还有这么多学生都能变成一模一样认不出来的火花，这说明——绘世学院的校园门禁系统该升级了！
t_en: If Sparxie could slip onto campus, and so many students could turn into identical Sparxies you can't tell apart, that tells you one thing. Graphia Academy's campus security system is seriously overdue for an upgrade!

t_ch: 唉，升级系统的事，实施起来可不容易。要是这件事能解决，那估计就解决了。
t_en: Ugh, upgrading the system? That's easier said than done. If it were easy to fix, it would've been done already.

t_ch: 楼道常备水枪和灭火器。
t_en: Keep water guns and fire extinguishers stocked in the hallways.

t_ch: 为了把《覆世沧浪图》的景画拼凑完整，你知道我找了多久的浪花、舢板和月亮吗？
t_en: Do you have any idea how long I spent hunting down waves, sampans, and moons just to piece together the scenery in Worldbreaker Waves?

t_ch: 这种防火装置实在是太古老了，建议常备高压水枪和灭火器。
t_en: These fire safety measures are ancient. Keep high-pressure water guns and fire extinguishers on hand.

t_ch: 你提示得很对。绘世学院存在不少消防隐患，看似很危险，实则一点也不安全。
t_en: Good point. Graphia Academy has plenty of fire hazards... That sounds dangerous, and in truth, it isn't impervious.

t_ch: 没啥好建议的。
t_en: No suggestions here.

t_ch: 没什么好未雨绸缪的，关于明天的事，后天就知道了。
t_en: No need to plan ahead. Whatever happens tomorrow, you'll know the day after.

t_ch: 嗯，嗯，有道理，你说话的风格和我越来越像了，我完全理解你的意思了。
t_en: Mhmm, makes sense. You're starting to sound more and more like me. I understand you perfectly now.

t_ch: 好了，实在太没得聊了，海龙主任的「废话很多」的采访到这里结束哩！
t_en: Alright, this is going nowhere. That wraps up our interview with Dean Seadrake, master of rambling!

t_ch: 请{NICKNAME}再选择一位记者接受采访。
t_en: Please select another reporter for {NICKNAME} to be interviewed by.

t_ch: 前排左手边第一位。
t_en: Front row, first from the left.

t_ch: 前排左手边第二位。
t_en: Front row, second from the left.

t_ch: 前排左手边第三位。
t_en: Front row, third from the left.

t_ch: 背景墙上那位。
t_en: That person on the wall over there.

t_ch: *小声*社长大人别忘哩！要给《狸狸周刊》留一个采访的机会。
t_en: *whisper* Boss, don't forget! Save an interview slot for Furbobo Weekly.

t_ch: 前排左手边第二位。
t_en: Front row, second from the left.

t_ch: 好了，各位亲爱的朋友们，今天的发布会就到这里哩！
t_en: Alright, dear friends, that's a wrap for today's press conference!

t_ch: 接下来我们还有一场面向星穹列车组全员的访谈，请大家持续关注《狸狸周刊》，所有采访内容都会发布在「乐园头条」栏目，我们稍后见！
t_en: Up next, we've got an interview lined up with the entire Astral Express Crew, so stay tuned to Furbobo Weekly! All interview content will be posted under Arcadia Alert. See you soon!

t_ch: 采访环节全部结束后……
t_en: After all interviews concluded...

t_ch: 啊…工位是我家，我不能离开家太久啵，求求浣熊大人允许我回去吧。
t_en: Ah... my desk is my home. I can't be away from home too long... Please, Great Raccoon, let me go back.

t_ch: 喜哉喜哉，社长大人，《狸狸周刊》现在运转得越来越好哩！但是…只有咱们狸几个，您瞧，实在应付不来这么多工作呶。
t_en: Great news, Boss! Furbobo Weekly is doing better and better! But... it's just us few furbos, y'know? We really can't keep up with all this work anymore.

t_ch: 该招聘新狸了！
t_en: Time to hire more furbos!

t_ch: 说的正是哩！不愧是社长大人，和小狸我想到一块去了。
t_en: Exactly! As expected of you, boss. Great minds think alike. I was just thinking the same thing.

t_ch: 该关门大吉了！
t_en: Time to shut this place down for good!

t_ch: 神马？！不可不可，我的意思是，我们该扩充人手，招聘新员工呶！
t_en: What!? No, no, no. What I mean is, we should expand the team and hire new staff, y'know!

t_ch: 该降本增效了！
t_en: Time to cut costs and boost efficiency!

t_ch: 对的对的，啊？不对不对，再降本增效下去，我真的要佛哩！
t_en: Yeah, yeah! Wait, no, no! If they push this cost reduction nonsense any harder, I'm seriously gonna lose it!

t_ch: 是这样的，《狸狸周刊》的人事部最近收到了很多简历，邮箱里都要塞不下呶！
t_en: So here's the thing. HR at Furbobo Weekly has been absolutely flooded with resumes lately. Our mailbox is practically bursting at the seams, y'know!

t_ch: 接下来，我们打算举行一场招聘会，请社长大人线下坐镇，面试一些有潜质的候选人，您觉得怎么样呶？
t_en: So, here's what we're thinking: we host a recruitment fair, and you, our esteemed boss, to grace us with your presence to interview some promising candidates. What do you say?

t_ch: 这就叫…BOSS特聘？
t_en: This is what they call... a VIP hire?

t_ch: 我喜欢，当老板的感觉来了。
t_en: I like it. I'm feeling like the boss now.

t_ch: 那小狸就开始筹备招聘会呶！我来约一下候选人们的时间，请社长大人徐徐等待片刻。
t_en: Then this little furbo will get started on organizing the job fair! I'll coordinate schedules with the candidates, so please wait around for a quint, esteemed boss.

t_ch: 一段时间后，招聘会如期举行，你坐在老板椅上，迎来了今天的面试流程……
t_en: Some time later, the recruitment fair begins as scheduled. You settle into the boss's chair, ready to tackle today's lineup of interviews...

t_ch: 甚好甚好，候选人们都已经在门外等候呶！社长大人来决定一下面试的顺序吧！
t_en: Excellent, excellent! The candidates are all waiting outside. Boss, why don't you decide the interview order?

t_ch: 1号候选人。
t_en: Candidate No. 1.

t_ch: 好的！1号候选人是…名叫「今天也要欧气满满」的欧克。这是她的简历，请社长大人过目。
t_en: Alright! Candidate No. 1 is... an orch named "May Lady Luck Smile Today." Here's her résumé for your review, boss.

t_ch: 你的真名就叫这个吗？
t_en: So that's your real name?

t_ch: 你有点落后潮流了呢，现在没多少人用真名了，这是人家的圈名呢宝子。
t_en: You're a bit behind the times, sweetie. Hardly anyone uses their real name anymore. That's my handle!

t_ch: 你的职业涉猎范围好广。
t_en: You've dabbled in quite a few jobs.

t_ch: 其实，我还在机铠抛光店、轻脚美甲店上过班。不过我听说一页简历上最好别写太满，才没写上的。
t_en: Actually, I've also worked at a mechatron polish shop and a lightpaw nail salon. But I heard you shouldn't cram your resume too full, so I left those out.

t_ch: 为什么流动性这么高？
t_en: Why do you job-hop so much?

t_ch: 这也要问吗？可能因为我是二相乐园第一爬墙运动员吧！
t_en: You really gotta ask? Probably 'cause I'm Planarcadia's number one parkour champion!

t_ch: 我觉得《狸狸周刊》需要的就是我这样的人才，老板你觉得呢？
t_en: I'd say Furbobo Weekly needs someone with exactly my talents, wouldn't you agree, Boss?

t_ch: 你很优秀，但我还需要考察几个专业方向的问题。
t_en: You're impressive, but I still need to test you on a few specialized areas.

t_ch: 会使用办公软件吗？
t_en: Can you use office software?

t_ch: 当然会啦！我给咖啡店录入过《第四季度销售方案.docx》，店长让我转成Word格式，我还自费开通了转格式软件的会员呢。
t_en: Of course! I once entered Q4_Sales_Plan.docx for a café, and the manager told me to convert it into a compatible word processing format, so I paid out of pocket for a file-converter premium plan.

t_ch: 大惊诧！你和店长当中有任何一个人是懂Word的吗？
t_en: Whoa, hold on! Does either you or the manager even know how to use Word!?

t_ch: 写过杂志稿子吗？
t_en: Ever written for a magazine before?

t_ch: 写稿子？这不是有专门的写手团队做吗？我做团播的时候，都是念写好的稿子啊？
t_en: Scripts? Isn't that what the writing team is for? When I do group streams, I just read whatever script they hand me, pretty much?

t_ch: 这这这…你好像对我们的工作有误解呶！
t_en: W—Wait, hold on... I think you've got the wrong idea about what we do here!

t_ch: 摄影水平怎么样？
t_en: How's your photography?

t_ch: 简直是VERY GOOD！我完全知道从哪个角度自拍最好看！这都是专门培训过的。
t_en: VERY GOOD! I totally know my best angles for selfies! I've had professional training for this.

t_ch: 我突然觉得一阵晕眩，不妙哩！
t_en: Uh-oh. I'm suddenly getting dizzy. This is bad, furreal!

t_ch: 社长大人，你看这位欧克小姐…？
t_en: Boss, what do you think about this Ms. Orch...?

t_ch: 委婉拒绝。
t_en: Politely decline.

t_ch: 很遗憾，我觉得你的工作经验和我们不是很匹配，所以……
t_en: Sorry, but I don't think your work experience is quite the right fit for us, so...

t_ch: …打住！像我这样的高自尊人群，是不会说出「我真的很想要这份工作」这种话的。
t_en: ...Stop right there! Someone with as much self-respect as me would never say something like "I really, really want this job."

t_ch: 请记住：是我拒绝了你们，不是你们拒绝了我，再见。
t_en: Remember this: I rejected you, not the other way around. Goodbye.

t_ch: 欧克哼了一声，不悦地离开了……
t_en: Orch lets out a huff and storms off, clearly annoyed...

t_ch: 决定录用。
t_en: You're hired.

t_ch: 不错，你完美符合我们的人才缺口。去找普狸策签入职合同吧！接下来，你就是派驻二维市的调研记者了。
t_en: Perfect, you fit our talent gap to a T. Go find Furbulitzer to sign your employment contract! From now on, you'll be our investigative reporter stationed in Duomension City.

t_ch: 这么顺利？老板你太好了！
t_en: It was this easy? Boss, you're the best!

t_ch: 悲哉悲哉，哪位好心人来替小狸上班，我觉得咱们杂志社好像要完蛋呶……
t_en: Oh, woe is me! Someone please come cover this lil furbo's shift... I have a bad feeling our magazine is about to go under, y'know...

t_ch: 欧克哼着小曲，开心地捧着录用邀请函离开了……
t_en: The orch hums a cheerful tune as she clutches her job offer and happily walks away...

t_ch: 那么，请社长大人选择下一位候选人。
t_en: Now then, boss, please select the next candidate.

t_ch: 2号候选人。
t_en: Candidate No. 2.

t_ch: 2号候选人是…是…住在隔壁的侦探先生？怎么回事，你要改行当记者呶？
t_en: Candidate No. 2 is... is... the detective next door? What's going on? You're switching careers to journalism?

t_ch: 改行是来不及了，但我也不知道为什么我的简历会到你们这里？
t_en: Switching careers is too late for me, but I have no idea why my resume ended up with you guys?

t_ch: 啊哩！我明白了，都怪咱们两家的办公室离得太近呶，我打印简历的时候…不小心把侦探先生的资料一起放进来了呶！
t_en: Ah! I get it now. It's because our two offices are way too close together, y'know! When I was printing resumes... I accidentally grabbed the detective's documents along with them!

t_ch: 你们这行业还挺严格的。
t_en: Your line of work has some pretty strict rules, huh?

t_ch: 做侦探也需要投简历吗？
t_en: Detectives need to submit resumes too?

t_ch: 当然了。首先，每天只是躺着是不会有活上门的。其次，我是个拖家带口的男人，家里还有一张嘴得养。
t_en: Of course. First off, work doesn't come knocking if you spend all day lying around. Second, I'm a man with a family. I've got mouths to feed at home.

t_ch: 真的吗？我觉得侦探先生躺在冰柜里的时候生意也挺多的，来咱们杂志社找人的，一半都是找你呶！
t_en: Really? I thought business was booming even when you were napping in that freezer. Half the folks who come to our magazine office looking for someone are looking for you, y'know!

t_ch: 好了好了，还有问题吗？看来是误会一场，没别的事我就要回去补觉了。
t_en: Alright, alright. Any other questions? Looks like this was just a misunderstanding. If there's nothing else, I'm going back to catch up on sleep.

t_ch: 等一等，我有问题——
t_en: Wait, hold on. I've got a question...

t_ch: 你给狸狸通信交过房租吗？
t_en: Have you paid your rent to Furbobocom?

t_ch: 冰柜全天开着，电费很贵的。
t_en: The freezer's running <unbreak>24</unbreak>/7. Do you have any idea how expensive the electricity is?

t_ch: 哎！资本…我就知道，当了老板的人，心会变黑。
t_en: Ugh! Capitalism... I knew it. The second someone becomes the boss, their heart turns cold.

t_ch: 这样吧，等我这个月的侦探委托费到账以后，你从这里扣吧，不够的下个月再给你。
t_en: Tell you what, once this month's detective commission fee comes in, you can take it out of that. If it's still not enough, I'll pay you the rest next month.

t_ch: 所以在我来之前真的从来没交过房租？难怪杂志社要倒闭了……
t_en: So you're telling me you never paid rent before I showed up? No wonder the magazine was about to go under...

t_ch: 你的期望薪资是多少？
t_en: What's your expected salary?

t_ch: 不是吧，你真的打算聘用我？
t_en: Wait, you're seriously planning to hire me?

t_ch: 为《狸狸周刊》的销量着想，我们单独开辟一个「罪案终结者」栏目，请他来撰写素材，订阅量想必也是极好的呶！
t_en: For the sake of Furbobo Weekly's sales, we could open a dedicated "Crime Buster" column and have him write the content. I bet our subscription numbers would absolutely skyrocket!

t_ch: 但我听说，只要侦探先生在的地方就会发生命案，雇佣他的事，我心里还是有点怕怕的呶！
t_en: But I heard wherever the detective is, a murder happens. I'm kinda nervous about hiring him, y'know!

t_ch: 讲个鬼故事，就算你们没打算雇佣我，这也会一直是我所在的地方……
t_en: Here's a spooky story for you: Even if you don't hire me, this is still where I'll be hanging around...

t_ch: 不接出轨取证业务？
t_en: You don't take cheating spouse cases?

t_ch: 小狸我知道！我见过很多以「丈夫出轨了！」为开头的工作函。
t_en: I know! I've seen tons of work memos that start with, "My husband's cheating on me!"

t_ch: …哼，真正的侦探会有犯罪的嗅觉，必须学会分辨哪些工作从不属于自己。
t_en: ...Hmph, a true detective has a nose for crime. You gotta learn to sniff out which cases aren't worth your time.

t_ch: 听「旁白」说，侦探大人因此见到了不少美丽小姐，但小姐们见到他本人之后，却一转诈骗他的念头，反而想要扮演「出轨的妻子」呶……
t_en: The "Narrator" says our dear detective met plenty of lovely ladies through this gig. But once they saw him in person, they stopped trying to scam him... and tried playing the "cheating wife" instead, y'know...

t_ch: 真正的狸猫应该懂得，有些话永远不要讲出口。
t_en: A true furbo should know some things should never be said out loud.

t_ch: 看来是没别的问题的要问了，那社长大人对不死途先生的录用倾向为…？
t_en: So, no more questions then. Boss, what's your hiring inclination toward Mr. Ashveil...?

t_ch: 委婉拒绝。
t_en: Politely decline.

t_ch: 侦探先生，你还是回去补觉吧。
t_en: Mr. Detective, you should head back and catch up on sleep.

t_ch: 太好了，小狸猫，请帮忙在我的门口挂一个请勿打扰的牌子，谢谢你。
t_en: Perfect! Little furbo, please hang a "Do Not Disturb" sign on my door for me, thanks a bunch.

t_ch: 决定录用。
t_en: You're hired.

t_ch: 开玩笑的，但我的确在考虑请你来写「罪案终结者」栏目的事情……
t_en: Just kidding, but I am seriously thinking about asking you to write for the "Crime Buster" column...

t_ch: 哈哈！等我人生中遇到的那桩最大的罪案被终结后，我们再来谈合作吧。
t_en: Haha! Once I've cracked the biggest case of my life, then we can talk about working together.

t_ch: 不死途掸了掸袖子，轻飘飘地离开了……
t_en: Ashveil dusts off his sleeves and drifts away...

t_ch: 3号候选人。
t_en: Candidate No. 3.

t_ch: 3号候选人是…哦？3号候选人有两位，分别是狸小龙和狸小虎兄弟。
t_en: Candidate No. 3 is... Oh? Candidate No. 3 actually has two contenders — the brothers Bruce Furlee and Tiger Furlee.

t_ch: <size=28>我们吃得很少！</size>（很少。）
t_en: <size=28>We eat very little!</size> (Very little.)

t_ch: <size=28>但干的活很多！</size>（很多。）
t_en: <size=28>But there's SO much work!</size> (So much.)

t_ch: <size=28>一定能包老板您满意哩！</size>（不满意退钱。）
t_en: <size=28>Guaranteed to leave ya totally satisfied, Boss!</size> (Refund if not satisfied.)

t_ch: 我去！好标准的简历。
t_en: Whoa! Now that's a textbook resume.

t_ch: 不得不说，你的简历一拿出来，所有二相乐园的人都要失业了。
t_en: I gotta say, the moment you whip out that résumé of yours, everyone in Planarcadia's gonna be out of a job.

t_ch: 可恶！你卷到我了。
t_en: Ugh! You're making me look bad here.

t_ch: 可恶！你卷到我们社长大人了…等等，这话应该我来说吧？
t_en: Darn it! You got our boss all worked up... Wait, shouldn't I be the one saying that?

t_ch: 哦，我忘了，我是社长啊？那没事了。
t_en: Oh wait, I forgot. I'm the boss. Never mind then.

t_ch: <size=28>看完了，老板还有什么问题吗？</size>（请问。）
t_en: <size=28>All done reading, Boss. Any questions for me?</size> (Fire away.)

t_ch: 你们两个是亲兄弟吗？
t_en: Are you two actual brothers?

t_ch: <size=28>是的哩，自古红蓝出兄弟。</size>（同一个狸猫妈妈生的。）
t_en: <size=28>Yep, red and blue have always been besties.</size> (Born from the same furbo mama.)

t_ch: 你们的说话方式…？
t_en: The way you guys talk...?

t_ch: <size=28>这是我们的性格锚点哩。</size>（印象深刻吧？）
t_en: <size=28>That's our personality quirk.</size> (Pretty cool, eh?)

t_ch: <size=28>就是因为我们喜欢这样说话…</size>（您猜怎么着？）
t_en: <size=28>It's because we like talking this way...</size> (Wanna know why?)

t_ch: 你们希望的工资区间是？
t_en: What's your expected salary range?

t_ch: <size=28>工资嘛，我们不在乎。</size>（给钱就干。）
t_en: <size=28>Salary? We're not picky.</size> (Show us the money and we'll get it done.)

t_ch: <size=28>但是…我们生下来就没分开过。</size>（连体狸说是。）
t_en: <size=28>But... we've been together since birth.</size> (So say the conjoined furbos.)

t_ch: <size=28>所以找工作，我们也要找同一份哩！</size>（嘿！）
t_en: <size=28>So when we job hunt, we hunt for the same job!</size> (Hey!)

t_ch: 哼哼，小狸我知道呶，所谓的7*<unbreak>24</unbreak>小时，其实是你们两个分开值班吧？
t_en: Hehe, I get it. That whole "<unbreak>24</unbreak>/7" thing is just you two splitting shifts, aren't ya?

t_ch: 两只狸卷我们一只，你们就是传说中的工贼呶！
t_en: Two furbos out-grinding one of us? That's just straight-up dirty, y'know!

t_ch: <size=28>你说得对，但不仅如此。</size>（我们两个加起来只要一份工资。）
t_en: <size=28>You're right, but there's more to it.</size> (The two of us together only cost one salary.)

t_ch: <size=28>老板忍心错过这样优秀的狸才吗？</size>（失之毫狸，差之千狸哩！）
t_en: <size=28>Can the boss really afford to pass up such excellent furbo talent?</size> (Miss a furbo by an inch, lose a fortune in fur!)

t_ch: 什么！？这…这真是绝杀，社长大人你看着办吧，我没招呶。
t_en: What!? This... This is a total finisher. Boss, you decide. I'm fresh out of ideas, y'know.

t_ch: 立刻录用！
t_en: Hired on the spot!

t_ch: 立刻录用！！
t_en: Hired on the spot!!

t_ch: 立刻录用！！！
t_en: Hired on the spot!!!

t_ch: 根本没有给我拒绝的选项啊，我马上让普狸策收拾出你们的工位，接下来你们就是《狸狸周刊》的常驻记者了。
t_en: You didn't exactly give me a choice to refuse, did you? I'll have Furbulitzer set up your workspace right away. From now on, you're resident reporters for Furbobo Weekly.

t_ch: 狸小龙和狸小虎欢天喜地地入驻了工位……
t_en: Bruce Furlee and Tiger Furlee happily settle into their new workspace...

t_ch: 那么，请社长大人选择下一位候选人。
t_en: Now then, boss, please select the next candidate.

t_ch: 1号候选人。
t_en: Candidate No. 1.

t_ch: 2号候选人。
t_en: Candidate No. 2.

t_ch: 3号候选人。
t_en: Candidate No. 3.

t_ch: 噫吁嚱！这趟招聘会，还是很有收获的呶。
t_en: Whoa-ho! This recruitment fair turned out to be quite the harvest after all!

t_ch: 咱们杂志社也是越来越好了，既然要给新来的员工扩充工位，办公区也该重新装修升级一下了呶。
t_en: Our magazine's doing better and better. Since we're expanding our space for the new hires, might as well give the office area a proper makeover and upgrade while we're at it, y'know?

t_ch: 本月KPI新增十篇发布。
t_en: This month's KPI: ten new posts published.

t_ch: 还是你懂我，这个月我们又能多发稿子了。
t_en: You get me! We can publish way more articles this month.

t_ch: 啊？我不是我没有，当我没说呶。
t_en: Huh? I didn't... I never... Forget I said anything, okay?

t_ch: 该团建一波了，今晚我请客。
t_en: Time for some team building. Tonight's on me.

t_ch: 走吧，去吃鸽川区最贵的饭！
t_en: Let's go! Time to hit up the priciest spot in Dovebrook District!

t_ch: 收到收到，等到了下班时间，小狸就叫一面包车把咱们一起拉走呶~
t_en: Roger roger! When it's time to clock out, this little furbo will call a van and haul us all out together!

t_ch: 一键升级！
t_en: One-click level up!

t_ch: 那么那么，为了庆祝新人入职，社长大人您看看……
t_en: Then, then, to celebrate our new hire joining the team, Boss, why don't you take a look...?

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>{NICKNAME}</color>进入<color=#dbc291ff>比狸比狸</color>的直播间……
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>{NICKNAME}</color> enters <color=#dbc291ff>Furblix</color>'s livestream...

t_ch: 哩啊家人们我就说今天起床之后我左脚先踏出家门过马路的时候每一步都踩在白色斑马线上相信这些生活玄学小妙招步步落实就一定会有好事发生哩
t_en: Furboooo! I just knew something good would happen! It started when I got up today and stepped out the door with my left foot first then made sure to only step on the white stripes at the crosswalk! I swear sticking to these little superstitious life hacks really pays off.

t_ch: 快看球棒浣熊真的来哩！
t_en: Check it out Baseball Raccoon is actually here!

t_ch: 老板啊我真的一直很想和您多点面对面交流的机会虽然不比那些杂志社的狸猫们和您朝夕相处我比狸比狸虽然派驻在鸽川区各处但我的心一直朝向您身边你不知道我现在多激动哩
t_en: Boss, I've been dying for a chance to get some more face time with you, I know I don't get to spend mornings and noons and nights with you like those furbos at the magazine, and even though I Furblix am stationed all over the Dovebrook District, my heart is always right there by your side, you have no idea how excited I am right now!

t_ch: 比狸比狸，请说慢点。
t_en: Furblix, please slow down.

t_ch: 比狸比狸，请说重点。
t_en: Furblix, please get to the point.

t_ch: 好的。
t_en: Okay.

t_ch: 欢迎家人们进入直播间，我们今天的PK机制主打一个「百无禁忌」。
t_en: Welcome to the stream, fam! Today's match mechanic is all about "Anything Goes."

t_ch: 也就是说老板和比狸比狸我啊我们可以在不违反异常防御部法律条例的前提下想干什么干什么只要直播结束前收到的火箭鲜花跑车飞机还有累计的人气值比对方多就算赢了哩！
t_en: In other words, you and yours truly can do whatever we want as long as we don't violate the Department of Aberration Defense's regulations, whichever side ends the stream with more Rockets flowers sports cars jets and total popularity points wins!

t_ch: 家人们我先开始了啊——
